title: Wednesday Sweet Spot Durability
objective: The week's key session, on the St-Jean holiday: three sub-threshold sweet spot blocks for sustained-power durability and soft FTP evidence, wrapped in extra Z2 endurance since the day off gives the time. Quality without the cost of threshold or over-unders.
why_now: This is a re-entry week after a manual-labor weekend, so the plan keeps a single controlled stimulus rather than the originally planned over-unders. Sweet spot sits clearly below threshold, absorbs cleanly, and tells us whether 260 W still reads right - useful since the working FTP is unvalidated and the whole 300 W path depends on it. A genuine fresh validation comes later, once re-entry confirms absorption.

source_context.week_objective: Re-entry and aerobic rebuild after a disrupted, manual-labor weekend
source_context.target_system: sweet spot durability + soft FTP evidence

targets.notes: ~2h15: 3 x 12 min at 88-92% FTP (~229-239 W), RPE 6-7, 4 min easy between, then ~1h steady Z2 endurance to use the holiday time. Sub-threshold and repeatable - third block as clean as the first. Not a test; cap is strict. If short on time or legs/back are off, drop the endurance tail (or a block) and keep it ~90 min.

warmup.notes: Progressive ramp; let the legs and HR come around. Add a couple of 30s openers near the top if you feel flat.

steps.notes: Sweet spot block - 229-239 W. Smooth and even, breathing controlled, no surging toward threshold. Cap climbs so they don't push you over.
Easy spin between blocks; let HR settle.
~1h steady Z2 endurance after the blocks - use the holiday time for durability. Conversational, smooth, cap any climbs. Drop this tail if short on time or the legs/back are flat.

nutrition.during: 30-50g carbs/hour and drink to thirst; fueling the blocks keeps the third one clean.
nutrition.post: 25-35g protein plus carbs within an hour.
nutrition.supplements: Today's workout needs: optional caffeine if tolerated and not late in the day. Daily baseline only if already part of routine.

recovery.post_session: Refuel, hydrate, left-calf routine if tight.
recovery.evening: Protect sleep - Thursday is a busy day with the interview.
recovery.next_morning_check: Thursday is a rest/mobility day regardless; just note legs and back before Friday's anchor.

skill_focus.action: Hold each block at even, relaxed power - rebuild smoothly over 5-8 pedal strokes after any climb or interruption rather than spiking back up.
skill_focus.why: Even sub-threshold pressure is what builds repeatable sustained power; surging burns matches and corrupts the FTP read.

mental_focus.why: One clean sweet spot session rebuilds quality without the recovery cost that would wreck a re-entry week or the busy Thursday.
mental_focus.reset: If the power creeps up, exhale, loosen hands and jaw, and drop back into the 88-92% band.

pre_interval_cues: Start only if back is fine, legs are normal, and sleep was acceptable.
Sweet spot, not threshold - cap is strict.
If the legs feel flat in the warmup, ride endurance instead.
during_workout_cues: Third block as clean as the first.
execution_notes.if_too_easy: Hold the cap - at most touch 92% (239 W). Do not turn it into a threshold test; progression comes later.
execution_notes.if_too_hard: Drop to 2 blocks, lower to 86-88% FTP, or convert the whole main set to endurance. A flat day after the labor weekend means absorb, not force.
execution_notes.success_criteria: Three controlled blocks at RPE <=7 with no fade in the third, finishing able to do more.
post_workout_capture: Did the third block fade or hold
